commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer-labeled stacked PR needs human contract and landing-order review plus current-head real CLI proof; there is no narrow automated repair finding.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff is TypeScript Doctor code and focused tests only. (sha=95b9f452633ff3fdf9dc1a6513d52640bbebaeb8)
commands.review_summary:
The branch extracts Control UI freshness detection/repair helpers and registers them as a positional internal Doctor health check with focused unit coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a Doctor repair conversion PR, not a bug report. The useful proof is source review plus live `openclaw doctor` CLI behavior on the PR head.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled stacked PR needs human contract and landing-order review plus current-head real CLI proof; there is no narrow automated repair finding.

target.title: Doctor: structure UI freshness repair
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the old broad PR2 with a small non-plugin doctor slice stacked on #85566.
- Extracts Control UI freshness detection/repair helpers from the legacy doctor UI check.
- Registers an internal core health check for missing/stale Control UI assets and runs it positionally through the doctor contribution list.
- Keeps UI freshness out of the broad structured repair pass so the existing doctor order is preserved and the repair is not run twice.
- Keeps plugin, gateway service, session, policy, SDK, and public health contract surfaces untouched.

## Verification

- git diff --check
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/commands/doctor.fast-path-mocks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/commands/doctor.fast-path-mocks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot: 4 files / 45 tests passed
- codex review --base fork/doctor-core-run-contract-migration: no actionable correctness issues

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Control UI freshness doctor repair now has structured findings and repair results for missing/stale UI assets while preserving the legacy note/prompt behavior.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 root durable worktree /root/src/openclaw-84290-doctor-next.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot
Evidence after fix: 4 files / 45 tests passed; core and core-test tsgo passed; oxfmt/oxlint/diff checks passed; Codex review clean.
Observed result after fix: UI freshness is registered as a core health check, excluded from the broad structured repair pass, run at its own doctor contribution position, and still uses the existing UI build prompt/notes for missing or stale assets.
What was not tested: Full repo check; live mutation/build of real Control UI assets; e2e-only doctor scenario because the local unit project excludes .e2e.test.ts files.
target.author: giodl73-repo
